title: IGLA-S Missile System
focus: Features, capabilities, and origin of the Igla-S surface-to-air missile system.
rationale: A distinct defense technology topic with specific technical parameters (IR homing, range, altitude) that are highly suitable for prelims statement-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: Indian Army received new Russian Igla-S missiles to counter drones, choppers, jets. About Igla-S: It is a man-portable, shoulder-launched surface-to-air missile (SAM) system designed for mobile ground units operating in high-threat environments. It is an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS). Key Features: It uses infrared (IR) homing to detect and lock onto the heat signatures of aerial targets. Range: It can engage targets at ranges up to 6 km and altitudes up to 3.5 km.
evidenceBullets: Improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS).
Uses infrared (IR) homing to track heat signatures of targets like drones and helicopters.
Can engage targets up to 6 km in range and 3.5 km in altitude.

title: Mig La Pass and Project Himank
focus: Geographical location and strategic importance of Mig La Pass and BRO's Project Himank.
rationale: Geography and infrastructure topic with high potential for map-based or Border Roads Organisation (BRO) related questions.
sectionExcerpt: Project Himank of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has constructed the world's highest motorable road at Mig La Pass, 19,400 feet above sea level in Ladakh. Road forms a critical link in the Likaru-Mig La-Fukche axis, connecting to the Fukche airfield near Line of Actual Control with China. Mig La Pass is located on the Changthang Plateau in Ladakh. Project Himank: The Project was raised in 1985 at Leh for the development of road communication in Ladakh region.
evidenceBullets: World's highest motorable road constructed by BRO's Project Himank at 19,400 feet.
Located on the Changthang Plateau in Ladakh.
Forms a critical link in the Likaru-Mig La-Fukche axis near the Line of Actual Control.
Project Himank was raised in 1985 for road communication development in the Ladakh region.

title: Zojila Pass
focus: Geographical details, connectivity, and the Zojila tunnel project.
rationale: An important geographical pass with a major ongoing tunnel project. Excellent for match-the-following or geography statement-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: Zojila pass is now open to tourists and does not require prior authorisation or a no-objection certificate (NOC). Elevation: Around 3528 m (11, 649 ft) above sea level. It is an important link between Ladakh and Kashmir, separating the Kashmir Valley from Drass Valley. Nestled on National Highway 1 between Srinagar and Leh, this pass is considered to be the second-highest pass after Fotu La. In 2018, the Zojila tunnel project was launched. When completed, it will be the longest bi-directional in Asia.
evidenceBullets: Connects Ladakh and Kashmir, separating the Kashmir Valley from the Drass Valley.
Located on National Highway 1 between Srinagar and Leh.
Considered the second-highest pass in the region after Fotu La.
The Zojila tunnel project, launched in 2018, will be the longest bi-directional tunnel in Asia.

title: Project Varsha
focus: Strategic naval infrastructure project for housing nuclear submarines.
rationale: Highly strategic defense infrastructure project with specific location and purpose, making it a prime candidate for defense-related prelims questions.
sectionExcerpt: INS Varsha, under Project Varsha to be operational by 2026. About Project Varsha: It is a classified naval project aimed at strengthening India's underwater nuclear capabilities. Objective: To develop a secure underground base to house a fleet of over 12 nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarines (SSBNs). Location: Coastal village Rambilli in Andhra Pradesh. Similar to Project Varsha from the east, Karwar base in Karnataka under Project Seabird safeguards the west coast.
evidenceBullets: Classified naval project to house over 12 nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarines (SSBNs).
Located at the coastal village of Rambilli in Andhra Pradesh (East Coast).
Complements Project Seabird located at Karwar, Karnataka on the West Coast.

title: Operation Black Forest
focus: Details of the anti-Naxal security operation.
rationale: A brief but specific internal security operation that can be tested as a direct factual question.
sectionExcerpt: 'Operation Black Forest' is the biggest anti-Naxal operation ever conducted on Karregutta hill situated along the Chhattisgarh-Telangana border.
evidenceBullets: Recognized as the biggest anti-Naxal operation ever conducted in the region.
Took place on Karregutta hill, situated along the Chhattisgarh-Telangana border.
